items.summary: Baseball America's latest Dynasty 500 rankings update identifies nine prospects who have significantly improved their stock through recent performance. The analysis examines what factors contributed to these players' rise in the professional prospect evaluation system.
items.pullQuote: Key risers demonstrate what scouts value in developing prospects

items.title: Fantasy Baseball 2026: Rankings, Sleepers, Dynasty & More

items.title: Top 500 Fantasy Baseball Dynasty Rankings For 2026

items.pullQuote: March performance data reveals updated long-term projection adjustments

items.title: #23 — Global Workspace Theory (GWT) and Prefrontal Cortex: Recent Developments (Baars et al., 2021)
items.excerpt: A wide range of experimental and theoretical studies in the field of consciousness emphasize the complex and rapid interactivity of numerous regions and connectivities in the cerebral cortex.
items.summary: Recent research explores Global Workspace Theory, which describes how the brain's prefrontal cortex coordinates complex interactions between multiple brain regions to create conscious awareness. The theory provides insights into how rapid neural connectivity enables higher-order cognitive functions and decision-making processes.
items.pullQuote: Complex neural interactivity shapes conscious awareness and cognitive performance

items.title: Office Hours: Will You Protest Tomorrow?
items.summary: Robert Reich poses a question to readers about potential protest participation without providing specific context or event details. The brief article appears to be soliciting reader responses about civic engagement decisions.
items.pullQuote: Soliciting reader responses about civic engagement decisions

items.title: The Catastrophe of Trump's War
items.summary: Reich analyzes what he characterizes as Trump's military conflict and its escalating financial and human costs. The piece examines the broader implications of current military engagement policies.
items.pullQuote: Military conflict escalating financial and human costs

items.title: Why Are the Wealthy Pouring So Much of Their Wealth into Politics?
items.excerpt: It’s simple logic, and the logic holds a deeper lesson — and some good news
items.summary: Reich examines the economic incentives driving increased political spending by wealthy individuals and corporations. He argues there's underlying logic to these investment patterns that reveals broader systemic implications.
items.pullQuote: Economic incentives driving increased political spending patterns

items.title: Why the ICE Raid in 'The Pitt' Matters
items.excerpt: The popular HBO show took on masked federal agents causing chaos for hospital patients and personnel.
items.summary: The HBO medical drama 'The Pitt' depicted federal immigration agents conducting hospital raids that disrupted patient care. The analysis explores how popular entertainment reflects and influences public discourse about immigration enforcement.
items.pullQuote: Popular entertainment reflects immigration enforcement public discourse

items.title: Two More Giant Ls for Trump
items.excerpt: In Iran, in the Senate, and in court, the losses are piling up.
items.summary: The analysis covers multiple setbacks for Trump across international relations with Iran, Senate proceedings, and court decisions. The piece tracks various political and legal challenges affecting the administration's agenda.
items.pullQuote: Multiple setbacks across international relations and court decisions
